name: Hodag
primary_sources: The Hodag: And Other Tales of the Logging Camps

visual_attributes.morphology: A large, bull-horned carnivore with a frog-like head and a dinosaur-like back
distinctive_features: Row of thick curved spines down its back
Grinning face of a giant elephant
Thick short legs with huge claws
Long tail with spears at the end
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: approximately the size of a large bull
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Head resembles a frog, body with dinosaur-like back, tail with spear-like end

functional_attributes.primary_function: symbol of local folklore and culture

domain_specific_functional.mythical_origin: born from the ashes of cremated oxen
domain_specific_functional.cultural_role: represents the spirit of the logging camps

relationships.description: Eugene Shepard was the creator of the Hodag myth
The Hodag is the official symbol of Rhinelander

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents local folklore and is a symbol of community identity
primary_sources: The Hodag: And Other Tales of the Logging Camps
